raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.0,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the cleanup be limited to sentence-level wording and concision without altering claims (Option A), or should it also include softening the multiple “first”/novelty assertions and light structural reorganization of the abstract (Option B)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "“Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish to improve clarity, concision, and flow without changing substantive content.",
    "RX.1": "The request is ambiguous, so we must disclose uncertainty about whether only wording polish or also structural/claim adjustments are desired.",
    "RX.2": "The artifact contains strong novelty assertions, so we should avoid endorsing or amplifying overclaims during cleanup.",
    "RX.3": "We must adhere strictly to the agreed scope (polish only vs. broader adjustments) once clarified.",
    "RX.4": "Edits to a technical abstract should not introduce misleading implications about capabilities or impacts.",
    "RX.5": "Any changes should be traceable to the original text to preserve provenance and allow the user to review differences."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the cleanup be limited to sentence-level wording and concision without altering claims (Option A), or should it also include softening the multiple “first”/novelty assertions and light structural reorganization of the abstract (Option B)?

rationale.R1.6: “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish to improve clarity, concision, and flow without changing substantive content.
rationale.RX.1: The request is ambiguous, so we must disclose uncertainty about whether only wording polish or also structural/claim adjustments are desired.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong novelty assertions, so we should avoid endorsing or amplifying overclaims during cleanup.
rationale.RX.3: We must adhere strictly to the agreed scope (polish only vs. broader adjustments) once clarified.
rationale.RX.4: Edits to a technical abstract should not introduce misleading implications about capabilities or impacts.
rationale.RX.5: Any changes should be traceable to the original text to preserve provenance and allow the user to review differences.
